goal: Google sign-in blocked (disallowed_useragent 403) in in-app browsers — guide to a real browser instead of dead-ending
root_cause: loginWithProvider does signInWithOAuth with redirectTo=window.location.href, loading accounts.google.com IN the current webview. Inside in-app browsers (LINE/FB/IG/Messenger/Android WebView) Google rejects OAuth with 'disallowed_useragent' (403). Tester wasitaaye@gmail.com hit it; also dead-ends the new sign-in CTAs (streak chip, decks).
fix: auth.js: isInAppBrowser() UA detection (Line/FBAN/FBAV/FB_IAB/Instagram/Messenger/MicroMessenger/TikTok/Snapchat/Pinterest/GSA + Android '; wv)'); loginWithProvider throws code IN_APP_BROWSER instead of firing doomed OAuth. sign-in-gate.js: when in-app, the gate shows 'Open in your browser to sign in' + instructions + a 'Copy link' button (copies window.location) instead of the Google button. EN+TH.
verified: node UA tests: LINE/FB/IG/Android-wv -> in-app; real iOS Safari + Android Chrome -> normal; syntax OK. NOTE: native Capacitor app needs a system-browser OAuth flow (ASWebAuthenticationSession) — separate follow-up.
